wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi اونٿ (avnṭ /⁠ūṭ⁠⁠/) (c. 1609) / اوٿ (avṭ /⁠ūṭ⁠⁠/) (c. 1565), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀉𑀝𑁆𑀝 (uṭṭa), from Sanskrit उष्ट्र (úṣṭra, “camel”), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *úštras. Also compare the Iranian cognates Pashto اُوښ (ūx̌) and Classical Persi…

def: n. m. S. उष्ट ; P. ushtar . 1. A camel. 1. Ūṅṭ re ūṅṭ, terī kaun sī kal sīdhī? Prov. O thou camel, hast thou one straight bone in thy body? (Said to one who has no good in him). 2. Ūṅṭ marā kapṛe ke sir . Prov. The dead camel's loss is recouped from the burden on his back. ☞ It is related that a merchant, who was lamenting the loss of his camel, was consoled by a wayfarer by the suggestion that he could recoup himself by adding on the price of his camel to the value of the burden, or merchandize he had been carrying. 3. Ūṅṭ ke muṅh meṅ zīrā! Prov. A caraway seed in the mouth of a camel! (A pinch of grain to a famished creature!). 4. Shahar meṅ ūṅṭ bad-nām . Prov. The camel has got a bad name in the town. (Give a dog a bad name and hang him). 4. Ūṅṭ chaṛhe, kuttā kaṭhe . Prov. Mounted on a camel yet bitten by a dog. (Misfortune has long arms for the unfortunate). 6. Ūṅṭ ke gale meṅ billī . Prov. A cat tied to a camel's neck. ☞ A man who had lost his camel made a vow that if he found him again he would sell him for a penny. In order to keep his vow and yet save his pocket, he tied a cat to the neck of the camel and he made it a condition that the purchaser of the camel for a penny should also buy the cat, which he priced at the real value of both. 7. Bāole gāoṅ meṅ ūṅṭ āyā, logoṅ ne jānā Panmeshar āyā . Prov. A camel to a village came, the idiots took him for their God. 8. Ūṅṭ jab tak pahāṛ tale nahīṅ ātā, jab hī tak jāntā hai, "Mujh se ūṅchā koī nahīṅ." Prov. The camel is tall till he comes by the side of a hill. 2. Met . A tall man; a fool; simpleton; blockhead. Ūṅṭ sā qad baṛhā līyā hai, samajh khāk nahīṅ . In height a camel, but not a grain of sense. ūṅṭ kaṭārā , n. m. A thistle of which camels are fond; Echinops echinatus . Wat.
s. m. Name of a thistle of which camels are fond. (Echinops echinatus. Roxb. ).

def: v. a. To plan; devise; contrive; provide for or against; invent; plot; scheme; to take measures. Phr. To set one's wits to work. Bābū, kāhe kahūṅ, kaune karahūṅ upāë? Jaunī upāë donoṅ mar jāë . Mag. Ballad ( Sarwan ). Good Sir, what can I say, or do or try? Some plan contrive by which they both may die. [The wife says with reference to the blind parents of her husband.]
v. a. To get an idea into one's head. E rānī jī, dil meṅ lī upāë, kis ne tumheṅ bahkāī? Marne ke nahīṅ din, maut kāhe kī āī ? Play. Rūp Basant .

n. m. S. उप + इ to arrive at. 1. Plan; scheme; design; project; device; invention. Bārā bachhar (year) kī tor miadyā, kaho, ka un kihis upāë? Bārā meṅ abhī do hī bite haiṅ, kaise chhuṭ ghar āë ? Mag. Wom. Song. Birha . Twelve years' imprisonment your term—what, whose the scheme? But two years yet have passed—how now escaped you home? 2. Measure; method; means; step; expedient; resource. 3. Remedy; cure; contrivance; redress; treatment; management. Bīṅdhā sakal sarīr, baid kyā kare baidāī? Koṭin karo upāh, ghāo nahīṅ det dikhāī. Dohā . I'm pierced right through and through, what can physician do? A million cures contrive, no wound to sight appears. 4. Strategy; policy; polity; stroke of policy. 5. Manœuvre; stratagem; trick.

def: v.n. To grow, be produced, spring up, shoot, sprout; to be born; to originate; to exist, be, have being; to happen, to become visible, to appear; to occur (to the mind), to be suggested, spring up (in the mind).
more: v. n. 1. To spring up; grow; to be produced shoot; germinate; sprout; yield. 1. Khet meṅ upje, sab koī khāë, Ghar meṅ upje, ghar ko khāë , Rus. Riddle. Ans. Phūṭ . 1. Melon, 2. Dissension. Grown in the field, who will, all eat it may. Grown in the house, the house it eats away. 2. Boyā gehūṅ upjā jau . Prov. I sowed wheat and reaped barley. [I received evil for good.] 2. To come before; appear; start up. Kar khusī, tero upjo dābedār! Rus. Wom. Rejoice, an heir is born to you! 3. To be born: to have being. Bāṛā bans Kabīr kā, ki upjā pūt Kamāl . Kabīr. Unlucky race, Kabir's, where springs a son,Kamāl. [ Kamāl (perfection), the son of Kabīr, used to make dohās (couplets), in refutation of those pronounced by his father. For example— Kahe Kabīr, do nāve chaṛhye, Ek būṛhe, to ekhe rahye . Kabir. Kabīr saith, always have two boats, for when The one goes down, the other serves you then. Kahe K…

more: prep. S. उपरि, Pr. avari above. On; upon; above; up; over. 1. Ghoṛā kasūṅgā, lashkar jāūṅgā, Tere ūpar saukan lāūṅgā . Song. I'll mount my horse and to the camp return, Another wife I'll bring, so you shall burn. 2. Kabhi ham ūpar, kabhī woh nīche, Kabhi woh nīche, kabhi ham ūpar . The Clown in the play. Now I am up, now he is down, Now he is down, now I am on. ūpar ā'nā , v. n. 1. To come up; rise; emerge. Pānī kā hagā ūpar ātā hai . Prov. Throw filth into the water and it will come up again. (Evil deeds recoil against the doer). 2. ( ūpar chaṛhnā ). To climb; mount; ascend; get up. ūpar ūpar janā, upar kar-ke jānā , v. n. To pass over; pass by; to be of no effect. Merā kosnā ūpar ūpar nahīṅ jānekā . Wom. My curses will not fall harmless. ūpar tale, ūpar tal ūpar , adv. One over the other; successively. ūpar tale ke bachche , n. m. Children born one after the other at intervals of one, …

adv. 1. On high; high up; upwards; up aloft; overhead; atop. 2. Over; over and above; in addition; in excess; into the bargain ( rūkan meṅ ). Jītnā ūpar, utnā hī nīche . Prov. There is as much beneath as above the surface. (He is twice as deep as he looks, from his years). 3. After; next; afterwards; after that. Davā ke ūpar pān khā lenā . 4. Against; in opposition. Fauj le-ke Rājā ke ūpar chaṛh gayā. ūpar ūpar , adv. Not through ( ūpar hī ūpar ). ūpar ūpar se, alvāyā, achhvāyā , adv. Gently; with a light hand.

adv. Above, on, up, upon, upwards, over, outside, at, atop, past. s. m. Top, outside, exterior. اوپرسي ūpar se, From above, over; as, طوفان اوپرسي چلاگيا t̤ūfān ūpar se chalā gayā, The storm passed over.
subs: adv. Skimming the surface; superficially; with a light hand, gently
adv. Upon, upon or after another, in succession
adv. From above, over, outside, externally, superficially; in addition, besides, to boot
adj. (f. -ī), Outer, external, superficial, &c. ( = ūparī, q.v.); higher, upper; superior, best; preceding, foregoing
To make a show of affection, to feign regard; to breathe one's last
s.m. Miscellaneous duties, odd jobs or services
s.f. Emoluments, perquisites, presents (and such-like illegal sources of income)
(apnī), To take upon (oneself), to undertake, engage, become responsible for

s.m. The Being above, The Supreme Being; the sky above; canopy of clouds; the moon above; — ūpar-wāle, s.m. pl. Superiors, betters; those in high position; the lodgers or dwellers in the upper story (of a house); those who have gone before, predecessors, forefathers; upper servants; personal attendants; ministers, stewards; outsiders, strangers
s.f. pl. (In the language of women), the spirits above, fairies, evil spirits; the fowls of the air, kites and crows
adv. On the surface; superficially, externally ( = ūpar-ūpar); apart, alone; secretly, quietly, stealthily.

def: postpos. (governing the inflected gen. masc.), Above, upon, on, over, on the outside (of); moreover, concerning, on account (of).
more: prep. Above, on, over. See اوپر ūpar.

def: s.m. Darkening; an eclipse; calamity; colour, painting.
v.t. To create, produce, cause to grow or spring forth, to cultivate.
postpos. (governing the inflect. gen. masc.), After (of time); afterwards; over and above, besides
more: adv. Afterwards; after which; else; besides. Do ghaṛī Māvas, uprāṅt paṛwā . Astrology.

def: s.m. A secondary mineral, as red chalk, bitumen, &c.; a secondary passion or feeling; a subordinate flavour.
more: n. m. A kind of red chalk; bitumen.

ety: S. उपरि + आ + छाट्, the towel being used to cover the body
def: s.m. A towel or cloth for wiping the body
more: s. m. A towel or cloth for wiping the body: adj. Superficial.
subs: (This and a number of other words seem to point to a Braj form उपरौं = उपरि).

ety: S. उपरि + आ + छाट्, the towel being used to cover the body
def: s.m. A towel or cloth for wiping the body
subs: (This and a number of other words seem to point to a Braj form उपरौं = उपरि).

def: adj. Upper, outer, &c. ( = اوپري ūprī, q.v.). — uparī man-se kahnā, To say what is not one's real thought or feeling.

adj. Upper, outer, external, superficial, hasty, cursory, not thorough, not sound or strong, &c.; unreal, specious, feigned; ostensible, apparent; belonging to another or to without, unfamiliar, strange, foreign; — s.m. Outsider, stranger, foreigner; exotic; that which is over and above, overplus, surplus; fees, perquisites (cf. ūpar-kī āmad).
more: adj. 1. Outer; outward; exterior; external. 2. Superficial; not deep; shallow. 3. Specious; artificial; affected; unreal. Uprī dil se pyār karte ho! 4. Belonging to another ( parāyā ). * Himself, his wife, and his ox. Uprī māl kī chāṭ paṛ gaī, ghar meṅ āg nahīṅ lagāte . He has acquired a taste for sponging: there is never a fire in his kitchen. 5. Strange; new; unfamiliar. Uprī ghar meṅ nīṅd nahīṅ ātī . 6. Foreign; of another country; alien. Kisī ūprī ādmī ko mat āne do . 7. Loose ( ḍhīlā ); not tight or strong. Sab ṭāṅke ūprī haiṅ . 8. Unbecoming; unsuitable; not matching.

n. m. 1. Stranger; foreigner; a new man; an outsider; one not belonging to the house, or the country. Kisī ūprī ko rāt birāt meṅ mat āne do . 2. One who cultivates land in a different village from his own (pāhī) . 3. An exotic; what is not native or indigenous to the soil. 4. Surplus; surplusage; overplus ( fāltū ). 5. Perquisites; fees ( bālāī ). 6. ( ūprī parāī ). Evil spirits; demons; goblins. Ise kisī ūprī parāī kā khalal hai .
(from اوپر ūpar, q. v.) adj. Foreign.

def: s.m. The being above or higher; eminence, exaltation, dignity, high position.
s.m. Acquiring, gaining, earning; acquisition, gain. — upārjan karnā, v.t. To acquire, &c.
part. adj. Acquired, gained, earned.
adj. (f. -ī), Outer; upper; exterior; superficial; — s.m. the surface (cf. uprālā).
more: adj. Superficial; the outside of a garment (opposed to the lining).

def: adj. & s.m. See اپرلا uparlā.
v.t. = اپاڙنا upāṛnā, q.v.
more: v. a. S. पट् to pluck out, S. and H. खन् to dig. 1. To root up; extirpate; eradicate; pull up by the roots; Dekhyo, bāpū, mircheṅ pāṛe lȳe jāë haë.. e-e ... Rus. Look, look, father! There he is running off with the chillies-Ho-o-o-o........ 2. To read; make out; spell; decipher. Chiṭṭhī kā haraph upāṛā, ke naīṅ ? Illit. jhāṅṭ upāṛnā,—pāṛnā , v. n. To pluck out the pubes . 1. Jhāṅṭ ke upāṛe murdā nahīṅ halkā hotā . Prov. A corpse is not made lighter by plucking the pubes . 2. Hamār jhāṅṭ pāṛ līheṅ ? Mag. Perhaps you would like to pluck—? An abuse. (I defy you).

def: v.t. To pullup or out by the root, to root up, eradicate, tear or pluck out, extirpate.
more: from उत् and r. पट Go) v. a. To root up, to extirpate, to eradicate.

def: s.m. A covering worn loosely over the shoulders; a scarf.
v.n. To be rooted out, torn up, pulled or plucked out; to be skinned; (see the tr. form upāṛnā); to come or rise up, to appear; to be marked, impressed, imprinted (with the mark of anything).
more: v. n. To be rooted out, to be pulled out, to be skinned.
v. n. To be imprinted with the mark of any thing.
To come to hand, to arrive.

def: v. n. S. पट् pluck, S. & H. खन् dig. 1. To come or rise up; to appear. 2. To be printed, impressed, marked; to be taken (an impression). 1. Siwāne tak khoj upṛā . Rus. 2. Chhāpā achchhā nahīṅ upṛā . 3. To be torn up, or plucked up by the roots. Ek kyārī būṅṭ upaṛ gaë . 4. To have the skin peeled off. Sārī khāl upaṛ gaī . 5. To be read, made out, deciphered. Mujh se yeh chiṭṭhī nahīṅ upaṛtī . Illit. jhāṅt upaṛnā, lit . To be plucked (the pubes .) To be of no consequence; to be no great loss. Kaun sī jhāṅṭeṅ upaṛ gaīṅ ? What does it matter?
s.m. Obstacle, impediment; disturbance; injury; — partiality; protection, support, favour, kindness.
s.m. Obstruction, impediment, obstacle.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Obstructing, impeding; disturbing; — impediment, obstacle, &c.
s.m. Chief priest of a Hindū temple.
s. f. Surfeit, mort de chien, cholera morbus.

ety: v.n. from caus. from उपरि+v.n. from caus. from rt. तृप्
def: s.f. Surfeit; mort de chien, cholera morbus.
s.m. A fast; fasting; hunger. — upās karnā, v.n. To fast, abstain from eating and drinking.
more: n. m. Māṛ. बास S. वस् to to live, dwell. A fast ( rozah ); fasting ( barat ); hunger. Koṭhī meṅ chāur, ghar meṅ upās ! Tir. Prov. Grain in the barn, hunger in the house!

def: s.f. Worship, &c. — upās karnā, v.t. To worship, make offerings to, sacrifice to.
v. n. 1. To fast; to go without food or drink. 2. To bring offerings; to worship; sacrifice.
s.f. Smell, stink; staleness, mustiness; putrefaction, rottenness.
more: s. f. Stink, staleness, putrefaction, rottenness.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Fasting; hungry; — one who fasts.
s.f. Service, worship; religious meditation.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā). Fasting, &c. = اپاسا upāsā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Fasting; hungry; — one who fasts.
more: n. m. 1. One who fasts ( roz-dār ). 2. A worshipper; devotee. (Walī.) Āp kaun deotā ke upāsak haiṅ?

def: s.m. (f. -ikā), A servant, a worshipper.
s.m. Patience, calmness.
s.m. Sitting by the side of; attending upon, serving; worship, adoration; religious meditation; — the sacred fire; archery.
s.f. Service, attendance; reverence, worship, adoration; offering, sacrifice.
more: v. a. To reverence, to worship.

def: adj. Calm, pacified, appeased.
s.f. Tranquillity, calm; — adj. Appeasing, allaying.
s.m. (f. -inī), Appeased, tranquil, calm; tame; — one who is calm or tranquil.
s.m. A portent, a natural phenomenon supposed to forebode future evil; a disease, possession by an evil spirit; (in gram.) a particle prefixed to roots, &c., a prefix; a preposition.
more: n. m. S. उपसुज to join. (Gram.) A prefix.

def: s.m. Sciences connected with or derived from the śāstras.
more: s. m. Sciences connected with, or derived from the Shāstr.

def: s.m.The right side or half of the body,the right: — adj.opposite.contrary.
more: s. m. Right (in opposition to left), the right side of the body.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Serving; worshipping; — a worshipper; a devotee.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Fasting, hungry; — one who observes a fast. — upāsī-paṛnā, To lie down fasting, go to bed hungry.
v. n. To go to bed hungry. Rāt paṛe upāsī, din khoje bāsī . E. Prov. He passed the night hungry, and in the morning he went looking about for the stale rice.
v.t. To serve, worship, reverence, adore.

v.n. = ابسنا ubasnā, q.v. v.n. To give forth a smell, become fetid; to turn; to smell offensive (meat or food); to rot, putrefy, become rancid or rank; to become mouldy, musty, or mildewed; to ferment; to become sticky or viscous; to become stale, or useless; to be weary, to be out of patience.
more: v. n. To become musty, to rot.

def: s.m. The organs of generation of a man or woman; the anus; the haunches; the legs; the arms.
part. adj. Arrived; ready, present; at hand, near, impending. — upasthit karnā, v.t. To summon; to present, &c.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Worthy of service, reverence, or worship; — an object of reverence, &c.
s.m. Heat, warmth; pain, trouble, misfortune; sickness, disease; haste, hurry.
v.n. To be tired, to be sick or weary of a business.
more: v. n. To be tired or to be sick of a business.

def: v.t. = اپاڙنا upāṛnā, q.v.

ety: S. उद्+पतनं, but cf. ubaṭnā and ubalnā
def: v.n. To run over, overflow, be excessive; to fall off, be wasted; to be removed, to be taken away
v. n. (dakh.) To be rooted up(?): see اپڙنا upaṛnā.

W. v. n. S. उद् out, पद् split. 1. To overflow (ubharnā); to run over the side; to be excessive. 2. To be moved, removed, struck (a tent). Bathān upaṭ gayā . Rus. E. The cattle pen has been removed from here. 3. To fall off; to be thrown away or wasted. Bhūn boyā, upaṭ gayā . Prov. E. The burnt seed came to nothing. [Said of a child who turns out ill].
s.m. Censure, blame.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Censuring, blaming; — censurer, &c.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Censured, blamed, accused, complained against; forbidden.
s.m. Grove; artificially planted wood (see upaban).
s.m. Minute description.
s.m. Fasting; a fast; hunger, starvation. — upavās karnā, To fast.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit उपवास (upavāsa). Doublet of اُپاس (upās).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Fasting, observing a fast; — one who fasts.
s.m. A class of writings (four in number) deduced from, and subordinate to, the four Vedas.
more: s. m. A division of Hindū science, deduced immediately from the Vedas. (Four Works are included under this denomination, and severally entitled Āyus, Gȧndharva, Dhanus, and Sthāpatya: the first was delivered to mankind by Brahmā, Indra, Dhanwantari, and five other deities; and comprises the theory of disorders and medicines, with the practical method of curing diseases: the second, or musick, was invented and explained by Bharata: the third upaveda was composed by Viswamitra on the fabrication and use of arms and implements handled in war by the tribe of Ksh̤atriyas: and Viswakarmā revealed the fourth in various treatises on sixty-four mechanical arts, for the improvement of such as exercise them).

def: s.f. Profane science (as opposed to sacred); inferior kind of knowledge.
s.m. A factitious poison; a narcotic; a deleterious drug (as opium, dhatūra, &c.).
s.m. The thread worn by the first three classes of Hindūs over the left shoulder and under the right arm. (See janeü.)
more: s. m. The thread or cord worn by the three first classes of Hindūs over the left shoulder and under the right.

def: s.m. Scheme, plan, means, expedient, device, contrivance, stratagem; way, course, measure; remedy, resource; preparation. upāʼe karnā, v.n. To devise, plan, plot, scheme; to try an expedient, &c., to adopt a measure.
adj. Contriving, remedying, &c. (see upāy)
s.m. An interview; present made at an interview.
s.m. Husband, lord.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Contriving, resorting to expedients, remedying; expert in the use of means; — an expert in devising, &c.
s.m. Application; employment, service, use; suitableness.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Useful, suitable; — a useful person.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Worthy, fit, proper, becoming, right; qualified; adapted to, suitable, appropriate.
s.f. Suitableness, fitness, &c.
to give instruction
a support; a pillow, cushion, that upon which one rests; affection, kindness; religious observance or obligation
s.f. = اپيکشا upekshā, q.v. s.f. Neglect; indifference; contempt; abandonment; trick, stratagem, deceit (as one of the minor expodients in war).
s.f. Neglect; indifference; contempt; abandonment; trick, stratagem, deceit (as one of the minor expodients in war).
s. (dakh.) Ecstacy ( Harris ).
= اپاڙنا upāṛnā, q.v.

ety: S. उद्+v.n. from rt. स्फाय; cf. फेन
def: s.m. Ebullition; froth, foam; effervescence.
more: n. m. S. उत + स्फ़ाय to swell, फण froth. Froth; foam; fizz; effervescence.

def: v. n. ū up, phen froth, ā'nā to come, S. फण froth. 1. To froth; foam; bubble; effervesce; to boil over; fizz. Dūdh uphan gayā . 2. To ferment; to become bad. Garmī se dahī uphan gayā . 3. To froth; fume; flare up. 1. Uphnī huī paṛī hai . Wom. 2. Yeh laṛkā sadā haṅḍyā-sā uphantā rahtā hai . This child is always frothing and fuming like a pot on the fire. 4. To pout; make a wry face; to be ready to quarrel.
v. n. To boil over.
v.n. To boil up, to boil over; to bubble; foam, froth, effervesce; to ferment; to turn bad (as milk, &c.); — to foam with rage, to fume. — uphan jānā, v.n. = uphannā.
v.t. To cause to grow, to produce, cultivate; to create; suggest (an idea); to fabricate, invent.
more: v. a. 1. To produce; grow; cultivate. 2. To suggest; to put an idea into one's head. 3. To invent; tell lies about one; to slander. Bāteṅ upjānā .
(trans. of اپجنا upajnā, q. v.) v. a. To produce, to make grow, cultivate.

def: adj. Fertile, &c. = اپجاو upjāʼū, q.v. adj. Fertile, productive, rich.
adj. Fertile, productive, rich.
adj. Fertile.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Producing, creating; — a producer, &c.
s.m. (f. -ī), Dried cakes of cowdung (used for fuel).
more: E. n. m. lit . What floats. Dried cakes of cow-dung used for fuel. uplā-sī phūlnā, lit . to be swollen like a cow pat. 1. To be swollen (the vulva ). Pahlī * hī rāt uplā-sī phūl gaī . 2. To feel the sexual passion very strong. Uplā-sī phūl rahī, dāb jāë koī .
s. m. Cakes of dried cow-dung.

def: obtained, achieved; available
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit उपलब्ध (upalabdha). Cognate with Gujarati ઉપલબ્ધ (upalabdh).

def: v.n. To spring or jump up; to rise suddenly (as a float after being pressed down under a fluid); to rise above the surface (of water), to float.
more: v. n. S. उत् up, पल् to go; H. ūpar ānā . To rise above the water; to emerge. 1. Laukā ḍūbe, sil uplānī . E. Kabīr The pumpkin sinks, the grind-stone floats! 2. Koī kahe gorī ḍūb marī hai, koī kahe uplāë . Song. (Lāonī) . 3. Chhudr nadī jas chalī uplāë. Tas thore dhan khal baurāë . Tulsī Dās. Ramayan. As swells a small stream quickly. so A low man's puffed with little wealth.

def: s.f. dim. of uplā, q.v.
more: s. f. Cakes of cow-dung of a smaller size than اپلا uplā.

def: n. f. Simile. See اُوپما
spectacles
adv. Over, above. (See next).
more: adv. (dakh.) Above, on high: see اوپر ūpar.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Upper, higher; uppermost, highest; exterior, outer; — s.m. being by the side (of), taking the part (of)
more: adj. 1. Exterior; external; outer; upper; outward; extrinsic. 2. Highest; uppermost; supreme. Uparlā phal do . Illit. uprā'lā kar'nā , v. a. 1. To side with; to take up or espouse (a cause); to take one's part; take part with; adopt; stick up to. 2. To support; protect; favour; countenance; second; back; befriend; patronize. 3. To interfere; come between; intermeddle; interpose. Jhagṛā to holā okrā se, tūṅ kā uprallā kare ailā ? Bhoj. The quarrel is with him. Why do you interfere?
s. m. Aid, assistance. اپرالا کرنا uprālā karnā, v. a. To take oneʼs part, to protect, to stickle for.
subs: (-kā), To take the part (of), to side (with), help, support, protect; to take part (in) officiously or obtrusively; to meddle, interfere.

def: Lit. 'Standing on the other or opposite side'; the west (cf aprauṭhā).
more: s. m. (dakh.) 1. The west. 2. See پري pare.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Upper, higher; uppermost, highest. — uprauṭhī koṭhṛī, An upper room.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Meddlesome, officious, obtrusive; intrusive; quarrelsome, wrangling; — a meddlesome person; a quarrelsome person.
more: E. adj. 1. Meddlesome; interfering; officious. 2. Quarrelsome; wrangling.

def: = ابسانا ubsānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to rot; to cause to ferment; to cause to become sticky.
(corr. apṭan) = ابٿن ubṭan, q.v. s.m. A paste (composed of one or other kind of meal, turmeric, oil and perfume) rubbed on the body when bathing to clean and soften the skin; a cosmetic; unguent
more: s. m. A composition of perfumes, flour, &c. used to rub the body with.
subs: To rub the body with ubṭan ( = ubaṭna).

def: = ابٿنا ubṭanā, and ابٿن ubṭan, qq.v. s.m. = ابٿن ubṭan
more: s. m. A composition of perfumes, flour, &c. used to rub the body with.
subs: v.t. To anoint and rub the body with ubṭan.

def: = ابٿن ubṭan, q.v. s.m. A paste (composed of one or other kind of meal, turmeric, oil and perfume) rubbed on the body when bathing to clean and soften the skin; a cosmetic; unguent
subs: To rub the body with ubṭan ( = ubaṭna).

def: s.m. Breast, bosom; heart
more: n. m. S. उपस् The breast; the ur lānā , Old H. for chhātī, yā hirde lagānā , v. a. To caress; fondle; hug; embrace; clasp to one's heart. Dekhtī, to ur meṅ lagā letī . Wom.
s. m. The breast, the bosom. ارلانا ur lānā, To caress, to fondle.
subs: — meṅ laga-lenā, v.t. To clasp to the bosom, embrace, caress, fondle.

ety: pl. of اعور aʻwar, v.n. fr. عور 'to be blind of one eye,' &c.
def: adj. One-eyed, monocular (syn. kānā); — naked, nude, bare ( = ʻuryān).
more: adj. plur. (of اعور aﻌwar ) Naked, oneeyed.

def: s.m. Lack, scarcity, want = toṛā, q.v. adj. (dialec.), Thy, thine( = terā).
s.m. A kind of pulse, Dolichos pilosus
more: s. (dakh.) A kind of pulse: see اڙود uṛūd.
subs: v.t. To throw uṛad (at one) after pronouncing an incantation over it; to cast a spell over; to enchant, exorcise
adv. (As much as) the white spot on uṛad, the least bit, the smallest particle.

def: v. a. (dakh.) To leave off, abandon.

u: اڑای ہات کی رسی خراب کرنا
r: uṛāʼe hāt kī rassī ḵẖarāb karnā
d: उड़ाए हात की रससी ख़राब करना
def: (dakh.) To empale.
s.m. pl. (of عريف ʻarīf, syn. of ʻārif, q.v.), The wise, the holy, those who are skilled in divine things; the intelligent, the ingenious; judges.
more: s. plur. (of عارف) The wise, the holy.

def: s.m. Snake reptile, worm
subs: (ʻsnake-eater'), s.m. (in Myth.) Garuḍ, the regent of birds
s.m. The infernal regions, the abode of the Nāgas, Pātāla.

def: v. a. (dakh.) See اڙانا uṛānā.
v.n. local, for الجهنا ulajhnā, q.v.
more: v. n. To be ravelled or entangled, involved (as the hair, or met. the heart); to quarrel without cause or unreasonably.

ety: उड़, rt. of उड़ना+āk = S. aff. āka
def: adj. Capable of flying; fledged; flier.
adj. Capable of flying; fledged; winged.
v. a. (dakh.) To steal, to filch, to rob.
s.f. The act of flying; flight
subs: s.m. A boys' game (resembling our 'ring-taw,' but played with cowries instead of marbles: syn. jhallā-mīrī)
(ćhū = S. द्रुत, 'presto!' or is onomat.), s.m. Flying away; disappearing; instantaneous flight
v.n. To fly away; to disappear, vanish, be non est
s.f. Trickery, deception, trick, ruse, stratagem; evasion, shuffling
v.t. To deceive, trick, gammon; to evade, shuffle
(-se), To fly (from), be averse (to), sick (of), tired (of).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀉𑀟𑁆𑀟𑀸𑀡 (uḍḍāṇa), from Sanskrit उड्डयन (uḍḍayana). Cognate with Nepali उडान (uḍān), Punjabi ਉਡਾਣ (uḍāṇ) / اُڈاݨ (uḍāṇ), Gujarati ઉડ્ડાન (uḍḍān), ઉડ્ડાણ (uḍḍāṇ), Marathi उड्डाण (uḍḍāṇ), Marwari उडांण (uḍā̃ṇ) / 𑅒𑅠𑅧𑅢 (uḍnṇ), Odia ଉଡ଼ାଣ (uṛāṇa). By surface analysis, اُڑْنَا (uṛnā, “to fly”) + ـان (-ān).

def: adj. Flying; raging, spreading, &c. (used in comp.)
subs: s.m. A kind of firework; a kind of rocket
s.f. An infectious or contagious disease; epidemic
s.m. The flying car of fairy tales which moves through the air at the will of the rider; a small ornamental rocking bed for children; a rough litter, a bier (of the kind used by Hindūs).

def: n. f. The act of flying; flight; aërostation; aëronautics. uṛān jhal'lā, jhal'lā mīrī . A game played with cauries or shells. uṛān-chhū ho jā'nā, uṛan-chhū ho'nā , v. n. 1. To fly away; vanish; run away; de- camp; bolt; slip away. Re, wāo, zabar, rau 1 , ho! uṛan-chhū 2 ho! havā 3 ho . 2. To be non est; to make oneself scarce. 3. To abscond; hide; to be in hiding; to elude search. Phr. To cut and run; take to one's heels; to sound or beat a retreat; take oneself off; take flight. Wahāṅ se uṛān-chhū ho gayā .

v.t. To cause to fly; to fly, let fly; to throw or toss up (as a pigeon, or a coin); to set free, uncage; to cause to move rapidly or swiftly, to drive hard (a horse, &c.); to fly away with; to run or make off with; to make away with, to rob, plunder, despoil of; to ravish; to cause to vanish or disappear; to squander, dissipate, waste; to put out of sight, hide, conceal (from, -se, as a secret, &c.); to use, apply, or handle dexterously, skilfully or effectively (as an instrument, &c.); to puff or blow away; to scatter, disperse; to winnow; to drive away, frighten away, scare; to blow up, explode (as a fort, mine, &c.); to destroy; to spread abroad, circulate (a rumour); to unfold, unfurl (a sail, flag, &c.); to give the reins to, give free scope to, indulge in (pleasure, e.g. mazā uṛānā); to knock off, cut off (the head); to strike out, wipe out, efface, obliterate; to put away from, cast to the winds, disregard (advice, &c.); to get rid of, send away, dismiss; to hurl or throw down (in wrestling); to knock over, send flying; to take off, copy, mimic; to play upon, deceive, cheat, befool, gammon
more: (caus. of اڙنا) v. a. To cause to fly; to squander or spend extravagantly, dissipate, blow up or away, disperse, filch, to entice. اڙانا پڙانا uṛānā puṛānā, To squander.
subs: ( = uṛānā, but generally intensive, e.g.), To carry off or away; to drive or frighten away; to give or cast to the winds; to wipe clean out, completely efface; to blow away; to blow down; to blow up, cause to blaze up; to explode (a mine); to get rid of, dismiss, &c.
uṛā le-jānā, v.t. To make off with, run away with, carry off; to kidnap; to entice away
v.t. To carry off, plunder, steal, rob.
ex: اس کا پورا سافٹویر ہی اُڑا دیا گیا ہے۔
is kā pūrā sāfṭver hī uṛā diya gayā hai.
The OS has been completely wiped from this (machine).

def: s. (dakh.) Device, trick, fraud, engine ( Harris ).
s. (dakh.) Deceitful trick, fraud ( Harris ).
See اڙانچهو uṛānchhū.

Sah. n. f. 1. Deception; imposition; make-believe. 2. Artifice; trick; trickery; chouse; humbug; blind ( aṅdherī ); ruse; feint; fetch; stratagem. 3. Evasion; shuffling ( ṭāl maṭāl ). uṛāṅ-ghāī batā'nā , v. a. 1. To mislead; deceive; practise upon; palm upon. Uṛān-ghāī batā-kar rupayā le gayā . 2. To evade; shuffle; put off. Jab māṅgo, uṛān-ghāī batā detā hai. uṛān'ho'nā , v. n. To be tired, disgusted, sick (of a place) ( uchāṭ honā ). Chalo, yāṅ se jī uṛān ho gayā. uṛā'ū,—khā'ū , n. m. A spendthrift; an extravagant person; a prodigal.
s.m. Breast, bosom, chest
subs: s.m. Lit. "Produced on the chest"; the female breast.

def: s.m. A bug.
v.t. To stick in, infix, insert; to prick, pierce, penetrate; to tuck in, tuck up, fold in; to stuff in, to poke in, plug

v. a. H. ṭhas thrust. 1. To stick in; insert; fix. Laṅgoṭe meṅ paisā uṛas le . 2. To pierce; penetrate; effect penetration. Tere uṛas duṅ . (An abuse). 3. To fill up; bung up; stop; plug. Bil meṅ logaṛ uṛas de . 4. To tuck in; fold in; gather up; roll up. Nefā uṛas le, dekh, pāëṅchā * laṭaktā hai .
n. f.). The sound made by the emission of wind from behind; a fart.

def: s.m. Extravagant person, spendthrift, prodigal

s.f. Fertile soil, yielding every kind of crop.
more: n. f. 1. Words without meaning; nonsense; gibberish; nonsensical talk. 2. An uneven road. aṛ baṛ bak'nā , v. n. To speak inconsiderately; to talk nonsense; to babble; jabber. aṛ baṛ , adj. 1. Uneven; rugged; rough (road). 2. Unmeaning; nonsensical.

def: s.f. Fertile soil, yielding every kind of crop.
s.f. Name of an Apsara or nymph of Indra's heaven; an apparition, phantom; an ornament worn on the breast.
more: n. f. S. उरस् breast, वस् dwell. An imaginary phantom.
s. f. 1. Name of one of the courtezans of swarga or Indraʼs heaven. 2. An ornament worn on the breast.

def: v.n. v.s.v. اڙنا uṛnā.

def: s. m. f. Thief: see اچکا uchakkā.
s.m. A kind of vetch, Dolichos pilosus.
more: n. m. A pulse; Dolichos pilosus , or Phaseolus radiatus . Wat. 1. Uṛad puṛad ke baṛe pakāë; Lāl lāl chiṛyoṅ ne khāë . Song. Ṭesū (Boys' rhyme). Fried were and done the cakes of dāl , The birds ate up the brown ones all. 2. Āp hī āp uṛad ke āṭe kī tarah aiṅṭhā jātā hai . For no reason at all he wriggles and turns on you, just like uṛad meal (which hardens very quickly while it is being kneaded). uṛad paṛh-kar mārnā , v. a. 1. To throw uṛad with a certain incantation in order to cast a spell. 2. To conjure; enchant; exorcise. uṛad pe safaidī. lit . The white mark on uṛad . The least, smallest, minutest, quantity. uṛ'dī, uṛ'dyāṅ W. Hin.; ba'ṛī, ba'rī , E. n. f. See adauṛī .

adj. Raised, erected, erect, upright; above, high, superior, upper

s.m. Going upwards, ascending; ascent; stiff walk or gait, walking proudly; — ūrdh-gaman karnā, To ascend, mount up, &c.
adj. Having the face raised, with upturned face

s. m. Deep inspiration, gasp.

def: s.m. An otter (com. form ūd-bilāʼo).

s.m. Army; camp; market of a camp; s.f. ( = urdū zabān), The Hindūstānī language as spoken by the Muhammadans of India, and by Hindūs who have intercourse with them or who hold appointments in the Government courts, &c. (It is composed of Hindī, Arabic, and Persian, Hindī constituting the back-bone, so to speak)
more: n. f. Originally , a camp. 1. An army; a bazār attached to a camp. Urdū lūṭ, Darībā lūṭā, lūṭā Mālīwāṛā, Guṛwāloṅ kī koṭhī lūṭī, lūṭā mandar sārā. Chhallā . A song of the mutiny of 1857. They looted Urdu, and Darībā, Mālīvārā too, Looted the Gurwāl's banking firms, and all the temples too. 2. The Hindustānī language as spoken by the Mohamedans of India, and by Hindūs who have learnt of them or have intercourse with them. It is a composite of Hindī, Arabic, and Persian—Hindī forming the base, and Arabic and Persian phrases, the foreign importations. — i-muā/?/l'lā . 1. The court language. 2. The Dehli idiom.

s. m. An army, a camp, a market. اردوي معلّيٰ urdū-i-muʼaﻌllā, The royal camp or army (generally means the city of Dihlī or Shāhjahānābād, and اردوي معّليٰ کي زبان urdū-i-muʼaﻌllā kī zabān, The court language).
subs: The royal camp or army (generally means the city of Dehli or Shāhjahānābād); the court language ( = urdū-i-muʻallā-kī zabān); the Hindūstānī language as spoken in Dehli.
wety: Shortened from اُرْدُوئے مُعَلّیٰ (urdūe mu'allā), from زُبانِ اُرْدُوئے مُعَلّیٰ (zubān-i urdūe mu'allā), borrowed from Classical Persian زُبَانِ اُرْدُویِ مُعَلّیٰ (zubān-i urdū-yi mu'allā, “language of the royal camp”).

def: exalted camp, royal camp
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اُرْدُویِ مُعَلّیٰ (urdū-yi mu'allā, “royal camp”). By surface analysis, اُرْدُوئے (urdū-i, “relating to camps”) + مُعَلّیٰ (mu'allā, “exalted”).

def: s.m. & adj. Craftiness, trickery, fraud, deception; — sloping, slanting, crooked, tortuous; crafty, deceitful, fraudulent
more: adj. Opp . of H. sureb' , or sudheb' . 1. Crooked; bias; zigzag; sloping; slanting; slant. 2. Deceptive; deceitful; tricky; crafty. Ham hī se ureb chāl chalte ho? ureb kī bāteṅ, urebī bāteṅ , n. Deceitful or crafty speech; specious speeches.
